CF 确实是互联网基础设施里最良心的公司之一 — 免费 tier 大方到离谱，DDoS 防护免费给，Workers 免费额度够个人项目用。    但你的担心完全合理。 CF 的"菩萨"和 Vercel 的"菩萨"本质上是同一个商业模式的不同阶段：    - 免费 → 依赖 → 锁定 → 收费   - CF 现在在"建立依赖"阶段，Vercel 已经到了"收费"阶段    CF 的锁定风险比 Vercel 低，但不是零：   - Workers 的 runtime 不是标准 Node.js，有自己的限制（无 fs、执行时间限制、特定 API）   - KV、R2、D1、Durable Objects — 这些都是 CF 独有的，迁移成本递增   - 你用得越深，走得越难    防锁定的实际策略：   - 业务逻辑层不直接调用 CF API，中间加一层薄抽象（但别过度设计）   - 数据库选可迁移的（Postgres/Turso 而非 D1）   - Hono 本身就是你的保险 — 代码不改，换个 runtime adapter 就能跑在别的平台    菩萨也是公司，公司的本质是盈利。区别只是 CF 的飞轮靠的是网络效应（用的人越多边际成本越低），所以免费策略可以持续更久。但永远别把赌注押在别人的善意上。

sample_tweets.text: Ok, now get back to duty and what is the response of old city incident?

I often compare infosec with firefighters, particularly incident response. Like, sure, running drills, working out, and playing pool at the fire house is fun and not too stressful, but when an actual fire happens, it's actually kinda fun even if it's terrible that it's happening.

Fire can be contained, but the pattern of risk keeps spreading. The deeper issue is not this incident alone, but how critical infrastructure is repeatedly drawn into conflict. Each controlled fire still leaves uncertainty behind. Lasting security comes not only from response, but from reducing exposure and breaking the cycle of escalation.   불은 진압할 수 있지만, 위험의 흐름은 계속 확산됩니다. 문제의 본질은 이번 사건 하나가 아니라 핵심 인프라가 반복적으로 갈등에 노출된다는 점입니다. 통제된 화재 뒤에도 불확실성은 남습니다. 진정한 안보는 대응을 넘어서 노출을 줄이고 격화의 순환을 끊는 데서 시작됩니다.

‼️🇨🇱 Zyght, a HSE (Health, Safety & Environment) software platform, has allegedly been breached with 6.1TB of data put up for sale on a popular cybercrime forum.  Threat Actor: igotafeeling Category: Breach Victim: Zyght Industry: HSE Software Site: zyght.com  The threat actor claims to have obtained 6.1TB of customer uploads and the full source code for Zyght's HSE software. The company was allegedly aware of the breach for weeks and did not resolve it privately.  Affected companies include major corporations such as: Codelco, Nestlé, Mutual de Seguridad CChC, Chilquinta Energía, SQM, Glencore, EcoMetales, Trafigura, Albemarle, SAAM, GHL Hoteles, Fénix Power Perú, Banco de Chile, Grupo México, Falabella, Fresnillo plc, Industrias Peñoles, ASARCO, Goldfields, and many more across mining, energy, banking, and retail sectors.  Exposed data includes worker PII and medical records, incident/accident files, identity documents, training and competency records, site security info, environmental compliance, audit and legal documents, and PPE/asset records.  Customer buckets listed include: Codelco (769.4GB), gssodet (629.1GB), Codelco-prepro (475.6GB), gsoch (283.4GB), Mutual (281.0GB), Chilquinta (232.1GB), Nestlé (201.9GB), SQM (191.9GB), Disal (163.5GB), and Goldfields (145.6GB), among others.  The data will be sold strictly once after a one-week deadline (April 10 GMT-3), giving affected companies a chance to reach out first, per the actor.

Experiencing some pretty hardcore burn out in malware.  However, a word of advice for the noobs, or less-er experienced people in cybersecurity, "burn out" is part of the natural progression of this ecosystem and it happens to everyone.  Your brain is a muscle (not literally, but brains have this dumb stuff called neuroplasticity, some nerd stuff, whatever), and just like a muscle, you need down time to heal, and science, or something.  Myself personally, I tend to go through waves of absurd productivity with little to no pacing. I get extremely excited, rip through code, ... and then lose control and crash and burn.  Then it takes me anywhere between a few days, ... or few weeks, ... or worse case a few months to recompose myself and get back in the game.  This is a good opportunity to switch it up a little bit. Instead of going schizo on malware, I've been exploring the internet, reading about current geopolitical stuff, and reading some psychology stuff.  I personally think it's important to keep "exercising" the muscle (plus I like learning), but some of my peers decompress altogether and switch to consuming high quality brain rot.  Anyway, the point being, if you've been going hard and suddenly you feel disappointed, or sad, or don't feel that "spark", or feel yourself struggling to even do a few lines of code, it is almost certainly burn out. I know some nerds are kind of hard on themselves, so don't beat yourself up if you feel this way. It happens to all of us (unless you're abusing narcotics to stay locked in).  Take this as a sign and use the opportunity to do something else. One day you'll be doing something and out of seemingly nowhere you'll feel that "spark" again and be like HOLY FUCK, I WANT TO CODE (or whatever you do).  Pic unrelated

My uncle used to work in IT back when distributing software wasn’t as simple as sending a link.  He told me a story I’ll probably never forget.  They needed to send a software package to a client team, so he did what everyone did at the time, He burned it onto CDs.  He approached it seriously.  Organized the files properly. Verified everything. Took his time to make sure nothing was missing.  Then he started the process.  One disc at a time, watching the system go through what he believed was the full burn cycle. When each one finished, he labeled it neatly (version number, date etc). Everything you’d expect from someone who cared about doing the job right.  He made multiple copies, packed them carefully, and sent them out.  To him, the task was complete.  A few days later, the responses started coming in.  The discs weren’t working.  At first, he assumed it was a mistake on the client’s end. That happens. But as more messages came in saying the same thing, he decided to check one of the remaining copies.  He inserted the CD, opened it…  and found nothing.  No files. No folders. Completely blank.  That’s when he realized what had happened.  He had gone through the entire process but never actually finalized the burn. The files were never written to the discs.  Everything else was done perfectly.  But the one step that mattered most was missed.  He told me that experience stuck with him more than any success he’d had.  Not because it was a big failure but because it was a simple one.  And from his experience those usually the ones that teach you the most in IT and life as a whole.

🤖 [GEMINI 분석] 2026-04-06 07:28 KST
🟡 위협 점수: 5/10 (보통)

### 1. 현재 주요 사이버보안 트렌드
최근 공격자들은 공급망 공격(Supply Chain Attack)을 통해 신뢰받는 오픈소스 패키지(npm)와 SaaS 환경을 침투 경로로 적극 활용하고 있습니다. 특히 AI 에이전트 및 멀티 에이전트 애플리케이션의 확산에 따라 새로운 공격 표면(Attack Surface)이 형성되고 있으며, 취약점 악용과 결합된 정교한 사회 공학적 기법이 대규모 금전적 피해를 야기하고 있습니다. 또한, FortiClient EMS와 같은 보안 인프라 솔루션의 Zero-day 취약점이 활발히 악용되고 있어 즉각적인 패치 관리가 필수적인 상황입니다.

### 2. 주요 위협 행위자 / 캠페인
- **TeamPCP**: 공급망 공격을 통해 SaaS 환경을 타겟팅하며, 최근 Vect 랜섬웨어 그룹과 협력하여 공격 범위를 확장 중입니다.
- **Kimsuky**: 악성 LNK 파일을 활용한 유포 방식의 구조적 변화를 시도하며, 이력서 및 대북 정책 문서로 위장하여 국내외 기관을 지속적으로 공격하고 있습니다.
- **STARDUST CHOLLIMA**: Axios npm 패키지 공급망 공격 배후로 지목되었으며, 개발자 계정 탈취 및 정교한 은폐 기법을 사용합니다.
- **Handala Hack**: 이란 국가 지원 해킹 그룹으로, 의료 기술 기업 Stryker 등을 대상으로 데이터 와이퍼(Wiper) 공격을 수행합니다.

### 3. 한국 관련 위협
- **Kimsuky 그룹**: 악성 LNK 파일을 이용한 파이썬 기반 백도어 유포 방식의 변화가 AhnLab ASEC 및 이스트시큐리티 ESRC에 의해 포착되었습니다.
- **RipperSec**: 한국 웹사이트 `icm.re.kr`을 타겟으로 한 사이버 공격을 예고했습니다.
- **자동차 부품 제조 기업**: NetRunner 랜섬웨어 그룹에 의해 인도 법인이 공격받은 사례가 보고되었습니다.

### 4. 주요 CVE / 취약점
- **CVE-2026-35616**: FortiClient EMS, Critical, **Active Exploitation** (Emergency Patch Released)
- **CVE-2025-55746**: Directus, Critical, Permission Bypass
- **CVE-2025-55182**: Next.js (React2Shell), Critical, Automated Credential Theft
- **CVE-2026-33509**: pyLoad, Critical, RCE
- **CVE-2025-7741**: Yokogawa CENTUM VP, High, Privilege Escalation

### 5. IOC 요약
- **CVE**:
    - CVE-2026-35616 ([TheHackerNews](https://thehackernews.com/2026/04/fortinet-patches-actively-exploited-cve.html))
    - CVE-2025-30208 ([SANS ISC](https://isc.sans.edu/diary/rss/32860))
    - CVE-2025-55182 ([BleepingComputer](https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/hackers-exploit-react2shell-in-automated-credential-theft-campaign/))
    - CVE-2023-32434, CVE-2023-38606 ([Securelist](https://securelist.com/coruna-framework-updated-operation-triangulation-exploit/119228/))
    - CVE-2025-7741 ([CISA ICS](https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/ics-advisories/icsa-26-092-02))

### 6. 즉각 대응 권고
- **FortiClient EMS 긴급 패치**: CVE-2026-35616 악용 사례가 확인되었으므로 즉시 벤더사 보안 업데이트를 적용하십시오.
- **npm 패키지 무결성 검증**: Axios 등 프로젝트 내 의존성 패키지의 출처를 재확인하고, 최근 업데이트된 패키지 중 비정상적인 동작이 있는지 모니터링하십시오.
- **사회 공학적 공격 대비**: 이력서나 세무조사 통지서로 위장한 LNK 파일 유포가 활발하므로, 메일 첨부파일 실행에 대한 임직원 보안 교육 및 EDR 탐지 정책을 강화하십시오.

### 7. 위협 수준 평가
- **점수**: 8.5/10
- **근거**: 보안 인프라(FortiClient) 및 개발 공급망(npm)을 직접 타격하는 공격이 동시다발적으로 발생하며, 실제 금전적 피해와 데이터 유출이 확인되고 있음.
